title: Consumer adoption compatibility and secure bootstrap
objective: Resolve the four canonical incompatibilities that blocked the first real consumer adoption, without weakening any boundary: separate the control plane no-paid-AI prohibition from an exactly declared product runtime integration inventory, separate the strict consumer CI contract from digest-pinned existing product workflows, model .claude/launch.json as an exact schema-closed non-instruction configuration, and add a one-time, replay-resistant adoption bootstrap that removes the initial protected-path approval deadlock without forging an approval.
prerequisites: CP1-A.1 merged into main and verified at c654fe1369f29b20e9e923d132b2d7aa90f9e6c9
no consumer repository change is part of this task
rollback_strategy: git revert of the merge commit; every addition is governance data, schemas and read-only validators, so no runtime or data rollback is involved
out_of_scope: repository ruleset and branch protection changes
product runtime wiring, migrations, deploys and releases
pinning actions in .github/workflows/core-ci.yml (tracked as repository prerequisite P7)
